 {"id":"bd-xsfh","title":"Implement ntm_check_available()","description":"# ntm Availability Check

## Parent Epic: bd-9o2h (NTM Driver Integration Layer)

## Purpose
Check if ntm is installed and functional before attempting agent-sweep.

## Implementation

```bash
ntm_check_available() {
    if ! command -v ntm &>/dev/null; then
        return 1  # Not installed
    fi
    # Verify robot mode works (fast check)
    if ! ntm --robot-status &>/dev/null; then
        return 2  # Installed but not functional
    fi
    return 0  # Available and functional
}
```

## Return Values
- 0: ntm available and functional
- 1: ntm not installed
- 2: ntm installed but robot mode not working

## Usage in cmd_agent_sweep
```bash
local ntm_status
ntm_check_available
ntm_status=$?
if [[ $ntm_status -eq 1 ]]; then
    log_error "ntm is not installed. Install with:"
    log_error "  curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/main/install.sh | bash"
    return 3
elif [[ $ntm_status -eq 2 ]]; then
    log_error "ntm is installed but robot mode is not working."
    log_error "Try: ntm --robot-status"
    return 3
fi
```

## Considerations
- --robot-status is a fast, side-effect-free command
- Should complete in <1 second
- Failures here should exit with code 3 (dependency error)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:48:50.860568631-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T19:03:01.746295971-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T19:03:01.746295971-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented ntm_check_available() with distinct return codes (0=ok, 1=not installed, 2=not functional). Updated ntm_is_available() to use it for backward compatibility.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xsfh","depends_on_id":"bd-6kme","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:58:19.033263139-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-y3vd","title":"Implement has_release_workflow() detection","description":"# Release Workflow Detection

## Parent Epic: bd-mkoc (Agent Sweep Command Implementation)

## Purpose
Determine if repo should have release automation (Phase 3).

## Implementation

```bash
has_release_workflow() {
    local repo_path="$1"
    local workflows_dir="$repo_path/.github/workflows"
    
    # Check explicit per-repo config first
    local repo_config="$repo_path/.ru/agent-sweep.conf"
    if [[ -f "$repo_config" ]]; then
        source "$repo_config"
        case "${AGENT_SWEEP_RELEASE_STRATEGY:-}" in
            never) return 1 ;;
            tag-only|gh-release|auto) return 0 ;;
        esac
    fi
    
    # Check user-level per-repo config
    local repo_name=$(basename "$repo_path")
    local user_config="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/agent-sweep.d/${repo_name}.conf"
    if [[ -f "$user_config" ]]; then
        source "$user_config"
        case "${AGENT_SWEEP_RELEASE_STRATEGY:-}" in
            never) return 1 ;;
            tag-only|gh-release|auto) return 0 ;;
        esac
    fi
    
    # Use gh API if available
    if command -v gh &>/dev/null; then
        local remote_url
        remote_url=$(git -C "$repo_path" remote get-url origin 2>/dev/null)
        if [[ -n "$remote_url" ]] && gh workflow list -R "$remote_url" 2>/dev/null | grep -qi "release\|deploy\|publish"; then
            return 0
        fi
    fi
    
    # Fallback: check workflow files
    [[ -d "$workflows_dir" ]] || return 1
    grep -riqE "(on:|tags:|release:|workflow_dispatch:)" "$workflows_dir"/*.yml 2>/dev/null
}
```

## Release Strategy Values
- never: No releases
- auto: Agent proposes, ru validates (default)
- tag-only: Create tag, no GH release
- gh-release: Tag + GH release + monitor Actions","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:57:21.4043331-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T16:57:21.4043331-05:00"}

 {"id":"bd-yk9p","title":"Implement ntm_wait_completion()","description":"# Wait for Agent Completion

## Parent Epic: bd-9o2h (NTM Driver Integration Layer)

## Purpose
Wait for Claude Code agent to complete work and return to idle state.

## Implementation

```bash
ntm_wait_completion() {
    local session="$1"
    local timeout="${2:-300}"
    local output exit_code
    
    output=$(ntm --robot-wait="$session" \
        --condition=idle \
        --wait-timeout="${timeout}s" \
        --exit-on-error 2>&1)
    exit_code=$?
    
    echo "$output"
    return $exit_code
}
```

## ntm Flags Used
- --robot-wait=SESSION: Target session
- --condition=idle: Wait for agents to be idle (not generating)
- --wait-timeout=Ns: Maximum wait time
- --exit-on-error: Return early if error pattern detected

## Exit Codes
- 0: Condition met (agent idle)
- 1: Timeout exceeded
- 2: Error (check error_code)
- 3: Agent error detected (via pattern match)

## Response Schema (success)
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "session": "ru_sweep_myrepo_12345",
  "condition": "idle",
  "waited_seconds": 45.2,
  "agents": [
    {
      "pane": "0.1",
      "state": "WAITING",
      "met_at": "2026-01-06T15:35:00Z",
      "agent_type": "claude"
    }
  ]
}
```

## Response Schema (timeout)
```json
{
  "success": false,
  "error": "Timeout waiting for condition",
  "error_code": "TIMEOUT",
  "hint": "Increase timeout or check agent status with --robot-activity",
  "agents_pending": ["0.1"]
}
```

## State Detection
ntm uses velocity tracking + 53 regex patterns:
- Velocity >10 chars/sec = GENERATING
- Velocity <1 chars/sec + idle pattern = WAITING
- 0 chars/sec for 5+ seconds = COMPLETE
- Error pattern match = ERROR

## Timeout Guidelines
- Phase 1 (understanding): 180s default
- Phase 2 (commits): 300s default
- Phase 3 (release): 600s default
- Configurable via --phase{1,2,3}-timeout","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:49:43.712151674-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T19:07:30.415581876-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T19:07:30.415581876-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented ntm_wait_completion() with idle condition wait and error detection at lines 6385-6408.  {"id":"bd-yrod","title":"Implement session cleanup and trap handlers","description":"# Session Cleanup and Trap Handlers

## Parent Epic: bd-kvu5 (Error Handling & Recovery)

## Purpose
Implement session cleanup function and trap handlers for graceful shutdown.

## Functions to Implement

### cleanup_agent_sweep_sessions()
```bash
# Kill all agent-sweep sessions for this process
# Respects --keep-sessions flag
cleanup_agent_sweep_sessions() {
    [[ "$KEEP_SESSIONS" == "true" ]] && return 0

    local pattern="ru_sweep_*_$$"
    local sessions
    sessions=$(tmux list-sessions -F "#{session_name}" 2>/dev/null | grep -E "$pattern" || true)

    for session in $sessions; do
        log_verbose "Cleaning up session: $session"
        tmux kill-session -t "$session" 2>/dev/null || true
    done
}
```

### setup_agent_sweep_traps()
```bash
# Set up trap handlers for graceful shutdown
setup_agent_sweep_traps() {
    trap 'agent_sweep_handle_interrupt' INT TERM
    trap 'agent_sweep_handle_exit' EXIT
}

agent_sweep_handle_interrupt() {
    log_warn "Interrupted - saving state and cleaning up..."
    save_agent_sweep_state "interrupted"
    cleanup_agent_sweep_sessions
    exit 5
}

agent_sweep_handle_exit() {
    local exit_code=$?
    # Only cleanup if not keeping sessions
    if [[ "$exit_code" -ne 0 ]] && [[ "$KEEP_SESSIONS_ON_FAIL" == "true" ]]; then
        log_info "Keeping sessions for debugging (--keep-sessions-on-fail)"
    else
        cleanup_agent_sweep_sessions
    fi
}
```

### map_ntm_error_to_exit_code()
```bash
# Map ntm error codes to ru exit codes
# Args: $1=ntm_error_code
# Returns: ru exit code
map_ntm_error_to_exit_code() {
    local ntm_error="$1"

    case "$ntm_error" in
        TIMEOUT)           echo 1 ;;  # Partial failure
        RESOURCE_BUSY)     echo 1 ;;  # Retry-able
        SESSION_NOT_FOUND) echo 3 ;;  # System error
        PANE_NOT_FOUND)    echo 3 ;;  # System error
        INTERNAL_ERROR)    echo 3 ;;  # System error
        PERMISSION_DENIED) echo 3 ;;  # System error
        DEPENDENCY_MISSING) echo 3 ;; # System error
        INVALID_FLAG)      echo 4 ;;  # Bad args
        NOT_IMPLEMENTED)   echo 4 ;;  # Bad args
        *)                 echo 1 ;;  # Default partial failure
    esac
}
```

## Integration
- Called from cmd_agent_sweep() at startup for trap setup
- Called on exit/interrupt for cleanup
- Error mapping used by ntm driver functions

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sessions cleaned up on normal exit
- [ ] Sessions preserved with --keep-sessions
- [ ] Sessions preserved on failure with --keep-sessions-on-fail
- [ ] State saved on interrupt (Ctrl+C)
- [ ] Trap handlers don't interfere with normal operation
- [ ] Error code mapping covers all ntm error codes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:55:10.439602142-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T19:25:46.054217374-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T19:25:46.054217374-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented setup_agent_sweep_traps(), agent_sweep_handle_interrupt(), agent_sweep_handle_exit(), and map_ntm_error_to_exit_code()","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-yrod","depends_on_id":"bd-hkmt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T18:04:46.932195647-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
